page.title: Electricity
page.description: Energy is one of the largest costs and environmental impacts in the accommodation sector.

validation.invalidOperatingDays: The year has a maximum of 365 days.
validation.missingFields: Please complete all fields in the tables: Operational Data and ENERGY (kWh).
validation.missingConsent: You must agree to data processing.

consent.label: By submitting, I agree to the processing of the provided data.

modal.title: Download PDF
modal.description: Your data has been submitted. You can now download the PDF report.

1.title: Guesthouse / Pension - only accommodation, no gastronomy
1.tooltip: Typically smaller facilities focused on lodging without dining services; lower energy use from kitchens.

2.title: Hotel / Pension - only continental breakfast, reception, basic services
2.tooltip: Basic services with limited food preparation; moderate energy use from breakfast and reception operations.

3.title: Self-catering accommodation, small accommodation
3.tooltip: Guests cook on-site; energy use depends on room occupancy and appliance efficiency.

4.title: Hotel with restaurant - full gastronomy
4.tooltip: Full restaurant operations increase energy use (kitchen, refrigeration, ventilation).

5.title: Hotel with wellness / pool / conferences - high consumption
5.tooltip: Wellness, pools, and conferences significantly raise heating, water, and HVAC demand.

summary.title: What we measure?
summary.whyText: Energy intensity directly affects operating costs and carbon footprint. Tracking consumption helps reveal savings quickly.
summary.quote: "Energy efficiency is the fastest, cheapest and cleanest way to reduce emissions." — International Energy Agency
